title: How old was Asuka when Kyoko died
display_title: How old was Asuka when Kyoko died
wikitext:
[]: How old was Asuka when Kyoko died?             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Apr 26, 2015 7:23 pm Do we have a range on this? Was it 3-4, or 5-6? I couldn't find anything on the wiki.

[]: Re: How old was Asuka when Kyoko died?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Apr 26, 2015 8:07 pm Clover wrote: I couldn't find anything on the wiki.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Apr 26, 2015 8:23 pm Asuka born in Dec 4, 2001, Kyoko died in 2005, so Asuka was still 3 unless Kyoko died after her birthday. But she wouldn't have been chosen to be a pilot at only 3 yrs old, so either she mis-remembers, or something is askew.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 27, 2015 12:39 am With Kyoko's soul already partly in EVA-02, from the contact experiment, it was inevitable that Asuka would be its pilot. The only point open to debate is whether being told that she was a pilot was an elliptic way of saying "Your mother has just died (and we've captured the rest of her soul now)." [ Resulting tangent . - Monk]

chunks.content:
Article: How old was Asuka when Kyoko died
[]: How old was Asuka when Kyoko died?             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Apr 26, 2015 7:23 pm Do we have a range on this? Was it 3-4, or 5-6? I couldn't find anything on the wiki.

[]: Re: How old was Asuka when Kyoko died?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Apr 26, 2015 8:07 pm Clover wrote: I couldn't find anything on the wiki.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Apr 26, 2015 8:23 pm Asuka born in Dec 4, 2001, Kyoko died in 2005, so Asuka was still 3 unless Kyoko died after her birthday. But she wouldn't have been chosen to be a pilot at only 3 yrs old, so either she mis-remembers, or something is askew.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 27, 2015 12:39 am With Kyoko's soul already partly in EVA-02, from the contact experiment, it was inevitable that Asuka would be its pilot. The only point open to debate is whether being told that she was a pilot was an elliptic way of saying "Your mother has just died (and we've captured the rest of her soul now)." [ Resulting tangent . - Monk]
